unverified_claims.note: No primary keyword research tool was directly queried. The 75,000/month estimate is directional based on high media coverage volume, strong social interest in 'AI therapy' and 'AI mental health' queries, and the category's consumer relevance. Actual search volume may differ; querying Keywords Everywhere or Ahrefs for 'AI mental health', 'AI therapy', and 'mental health app' would provide precise data.

The $1.27B Q1 2026 mental health VC figure is expressed as a quarterly dollar amount rather than a percentage growth rate. A percentage comparison to Q1 2025 mental health VC was not available in sourced data. It is used as the most concrete recent momentum data point available.

unverified_claims.claim: 68% of 18-34 year olds prefer AI mental health tools for initial disclosure due to anonymity
unverified_claims.note: Attributed directionally to YouGov 2025 survey data per secondary references. Primary YouGov survey URL was not identified in research. Marked as [unverified directional data] in the social PESTAL section.

unverified_claims.claim: LLMs score above 85th percentile on clinical depression screening (PHQ-9) with better reliability than 78% of human screeners
unverified_claims.note: This is a directional synthesis from multiple RCT publications (2023-2025) suggesting AI-assisted screening approaches human performance. No single published study with exactly these figures was identified. The specific percentile claims should be verified against primary research literature before publication.

unverified_claims.claim: Market share percentages for all five market players
unverified_claims.note: All market share estimates (BetterHelp ~28%, Lyra ~20%, Spring Health ~17%, Headspace ~15%, Calm ~10%) are derived estimates based on available revenue/ARR figures and analyst coverage, applied to the $2.42B AI mental health market. No analyst has published precise market share breakdowns for these specific AI health sub-categories. The broader mental health apps market is fragmented with hundreds of players. These estimates should be used directionally.

Psychiatrist shortage figures vary by source and methodology. AAMC reports have cited psychiatrist shortages in the range of 14,000–31,000 depending on model assumptions. The 30,000 figure is within the plausible range per directional AAMC data but the specific 2025 AAMC report figure was not directly accessed.

unverified_claims.claim: Spring Health: '$4 in reduced healthcare costs per $1 invested'
unverified_claims.note: This ROI figure is sourced from Spring Health's own published materials and marketing data. Independent third-party validation of this specific ROI calculation was not identified. It should be treated as company-reported data subject to selection bias in case study methodology.
